title: Lease item (GET /leases)
description: One active task lease, in the viewer-safe leasePublic projection ONLY. As-built source of truth: apps/manager/docs/contracts/schemas/queue-lease.schema.json $defs/leasePublic (backed by the tracker's claimed_by/lease_expires_at/fence columns). claim_token is a capability SECRET and NEVER appears in any console read — knowing worker/expiry/fence enables no action without the token; the console uses them for swimlanes and lease countdowns. Filters: `worker`, `task_id`, plus the shared limit/cursor/since. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields.

schema_version.description: Queue-lease contract version (passed through from leasePublic).

task_id.description: Tracker task id (tasks.id) this lease is held on.

worker.description: Canonical lowercase handle of the claiming agent (tasks.claimed_by).

fence.description: Monotonic per-task fencing token, incremented on every successful (re)claim; the store rejects writes carrying a stale fence.

granted_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when this claim won.

lease_expires_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — after this instant the lease is reapable (doing->todo, lease fields cleared).

lease_seconds.description: Lease duration granted (default 30 minutes); renewals extend lease_expires_at by this much.
